name: Meira - Birth of the Miño River Return
name_original: Meira - Nacimiento del Río Miño Vuelta
region_intro: Road cycling around Meira offers diverse landscapes, from coastal roads to challenging climbs through natural parks. The region features varied terrain, including the dramatic limestone cliffs and Mediterranean vegetation of Arrábida Natural Park, as well as serene tracks. Cyclists can navigate routes with significant elevation changes and fast descents, providing a range of experiences. This area is characterized by its blend of mountainous and coastal features, making it suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Meira The most popular road cycling route is Meira - Birth of the Miño River Return, a 24.3 miles (39.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route features notable elevation gains along its course. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cádavo, Baleira – Alto da Baqueriza loop from Meira, a difficult 38.4 miles (61.7 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and descents, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Paradavella Inn – Alto da Baqueriza loop from Meira, a 58.4 miles (93.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation, often completed in about 5 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Meira is defined by its coastal roads, challenging climbs, and diverse natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding routes with considerable elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 150 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Meira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Meira?
A: Meira offers a selection of around 30 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from coastal roads to challenging climbs through natural parks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Meira?
A: The region around Meira provides a varied cycling experience. You'll find a mix of coastal roads offering stunning views, challenging climbs through natural parks like Serra da Arrábida, and serene tracks. The terrain includes dramatic limestone cliffs, Mediterranean vegetation, and roads with significant elevation changes and fast descents.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Meira?
A: Yes, Meira is known for its challenging routes with substantial ascents. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradavella Inn – Alto da Baqueriza loop from Meira&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 93.9 km trail with over 2,100 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Meira?
A: While many routes in Meira feature notable elevation, there are moderate options suitable for those looking for less extreme challenges. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939130046/meira-birth-of-the-mino-river-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meira - Birth of the Miño River Return&lt;/a&gt;, at 39.1 km, offer a moderate experience with manageable elevation gains.

Q: What are the longest road cycling routes available in the Meira area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Meira offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradavella Inn – Alto da Baqueriza loop from Meira&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, spanning 93.9 km. Another significant option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradavella Inn – Cádavo, Baleira loop from Meira&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 76.9 km.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Meira?
A: Road cycling routes around Meira can lead you to several interesting natural and historical sites. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedregal de Irimia – Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt;. The broader region, including Arrábida Natural Park, offers stunning coastal views and unique landscapes.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike in Meira?
A: Yes, you can combine your ride with visits to historical and cultural sites. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Viladonga Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Meira&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4862954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de Riotorto&lt;/a&gt;. These offer a glimpse into the region's rich heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Meira?
A: The Sesimbra region, which encompasses Meira, is popular for cycling in May and August. Generally, spring and early autumn offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded roads, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes without the peak summer heat or winter chill.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Meira?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Meira are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cádavo, Baleira – Alto da Baqueriza loop from Meira&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Meira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Meira suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While many routes in Meira feature significant elevation, the region offers diverse terrain. Families with older children or those comfortable with moderate climbs might enjoy some of the shorter, less demanding routes. It's advisable to check the elevation profiles and distances of specific routes to ensure they match your family's fitness level.

Q: What makes road cycling in Meira unique compared to other regions?
A: Meira's uniqueness lies in its blend of dramatic coastal scenery, the challenging climbs of the Arrábida Natural Park, and serene tracks through Mediterranean vegetation. The area offers a distinct microclimate and roads that are often car-free, especially within the natural park, providing a peaceful and immersive cycling experience with stunning Atlantic views.
tips: XII century monastery located in the main square of Meira. A good place to eat and drink throughout your trip.
Steep climb from Meira to the source of the River Miño. It is worth the effort to see where this mighty river begins.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Santa María de Meira
